channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Senate heads toward confirming Noem as Trump's homeland security secretary
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Senate is heading toward a vote on confirming Kristi Noem as homeland security secretary, putting the South Dakota governor in charge of a sprawling agency that is essential to national security and President Donald Trump's plans to clamp down on illegal immigration.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate puts Noem on doorstep of DHS confirmation
item.description: Noem’s confirmation would put the final piece of President Trump’s core national security team in place.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's border emergency declaration comes amid relative calm after years of major turmoil
item.description: President Donald Trump has declared a national emergency on the border with Mexico, saying “America’s sovereignty is under attack.”.
item.dc:creator: Elliot Spagat, Gregory Bull And Eugene Garcia, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Democratic states weigh more support for immigrants as Trump administration cracks down
item.description: As President Donald Trump cracks down on immigration, lawmakers in some Democratic-led states are proposing new ways to resist his efforts.
item.dc:creator: David A. Lieb, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Just days into the new Trump administration, worries spike amid a show of force on immigration
item.description: NEWARK, N.J. (AP) — Less than a week into his presidency, the Trump administration touted deportation efforts and published new rules Friday making it easier to remove people — part of a flurry of actions to make good on campaign promises to crack down on illegal immigration.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CATALINI and REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump reinstates Mexico City Policy, separates taxpayer dollars and abortions
item.description: President Trump Friday signed an executive order reinstating the Mexico City Policy, which forbids using taxpayer money for coercive abortion or involuntary sterilization by NGOs.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pete Hegseth confirmed to lead Pentagon after VP Vance casts tie-breaking vote
item.description: The Senate voted Friday night on the confirmation of Trump's pick to lead the Department of Defense, Pete Hegseth.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Kern County reacts: Trump proposes voter ID mandate for California elections
item.description: Back when 17 News covered that story, we asked viewers if they thought voter ID should be required. More than 700 voted, and 92% said voter ID should be checked at the polls.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Huh, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump Department of Education dismisses 'meritless' book ban complaints
item.description: Several "book ban" complaints issued during the Biden administration have been dismissed by the U.S. Department of Education, officials said Friday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: President Trump names Australian professional basketball team owner as New Zealand, Samoa ambassador
item.description: President Trump named Jared Novelly, Illawarra Hawks basketball team owner in Australia, as ambassador to New Zealand and the Independent State of Samoa.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NY Congresswoman Laura Gillen explains Laken Riley Act
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – The new U.S. House of Representatives passed its first major, bipartisan legislation this week. The Laken Riley Act requires federal officials to detain undocumented immigrants if they are accused of theft.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: St. Paul City Council President Mitra Jalali announces resignation, citing health concerns
item.description: After nearly seven years with the city, St. Paul council member and president Mitra Jalali has decided to step down. She says she is focusing on prioritizing her mental and physical health.
item.dc:creator: Sam Stroozas, MPR News

item.title: Federal agencies begin removing DEI guidance from websites in Trump crackdown
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Federal agencies have begun removing resources for underrepresented Americans from their webpages following President Donald Trump's executive order cracking down on diversity, equity and inclusion initiatives.
item.dc:creator: ALIA WONG and ANNIE MA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cruz spearheads effort to derail nuclear waste dumping in oil-rich area of Texas
item.description: Sen. Ted Cruz, R-Texas, is urging the Supreme Court to uphold a ruling holding the Nuclear Regulatory Commission lacks licensing authority, arguing that nuclear waste sites in Texas pose "an enormous threat to the country’s security and economic well-being.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hearing set for Kash Patel nomination to lead FBI
item.description: Kash Patel, President Trump’s nominee to lead the FBI, is expected to appear before the Senate Judiciary Committee on Thursday. Patel held numerous national security roles during the first Trump administration but would be a new face at the FBI.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Who's the guy handing Trump those binders of executive orders? Meet Will Scharf
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Those binders full of executive orders that President Donald Trump has been signing with a flourish and a wide-tipped Sharpie during his first week in office don't just magically appear before him.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 1,300 arrested in big city deportation operations: Homan
item.description: Number in first three days of Trump’s presidency represents about 1.1% of total arrests made by ICE in FY2024.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Trump ‘very surprised’ by Murkowski, Collins votes against Hegseth
item.description: President Donald Trump said Friday that he wasn't expecting Republican Sens. Lisa Murkowski (Alaska) and Susan Collins (Maine) to vote against his controversial nominee for secretary of Defense, Army veteran and former Fox News host Pete Hegseth.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump has tossed a lot of Biden policies. But there’s one AI measure he wants to keep
item.description: President Trump says he likes Biden’s idea to open up federal lands for AI data centers. His White House is looking for ways to ensure U.S. dominance in the sector.
item.dc:creator: Deepa Shivaram, MPR News

item.title: White House says migrant deportation flights with military aircraft have begun
item.description: The Trump administration has started flying immigrants who entered the U.S. illegally out of the country using military aircraft, a White House spokesperson said Friday.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tenn. Republican proposes amendment to allow Trump to serve 3rd term
item.description: Like other U.S. presidents, Trump is barred from running for a third White House term by the 22nd Amendment.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota justices tell lawmakers to work out control of ‘dysfunctional’ House
item.description: The Minnesota Supreme Court on Friday agreed with DFLers that it takes 68 votes to conduct House business, but the opinion didn’t come with a road map on how the House should proceed from here.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson and Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Trump orders release of JFK, RFK and MLK assassination records
item.description: President Donald Trump has ordered the release of thousands of classified governmental documents about the 1963 assassination of President John F. Kennedy, which has fueled conspiracy theories for decades.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Walz proposes $887 million bonding bill for infrastructure projects, not worried about Capitol impasse
item.description: Using the breached Rapidan Dam as a backdrop Gov. Tim Walz pitched his $887 million bonding bill. As well as funding for infrastructure, Walz wants to fund a new state patrol headquarters, and address housing and environmental needs.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Yang, MPR News

item.title: Hegseth told senator he paid $50,000 to woman who accused him of 2017 sex assault
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Pete Hegseth, President Donald Trump’s nominee for defense secretary, paid $50,000 to the woman who accused him of sexual assault in 2017, according to answers he provided to a senator during his confirmation process that The Associated Press has obtained.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: US active duty troops beginning to arrive in Texas, San Diego to support border security
item.description: Active duty military troops will begin arriving in El Paso, Texas, and San Diego on Thursday evening, in what defense officials said is the first batch of the new forces being deployed to secure the southern border.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Federal judge temporarily blocks Trump’s executive order ending birthright citizenship
item.description: A federal judge on Thursday temporarily blocked President Donald Trump’s executive order ending the constitutional guarantee of birthright citizenship regardless of the parents’ immigration status.
item.dc:creator: EUGENE JOHNSON and MIKE CATALINI Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump calls DEI programs ‘illegal’ and ‘immoral.’ Here’s how he’s ending them
item.description: President Donald Trump has issued sweeping executive actions swiftly ending diversity, equity, inclusion and accessibility programs within the federal government. Already, the work is underway.
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu, MPR News

item.title: Everything Trump did in the first executive orders and actions of his presidency
item.description: President Donald Trump began his second administration with a blitz of policy actions to reorient U.S. government priorities. Some have an immediate policy impact. Others are more symbolic.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Bishop Mariann Budde, former Minnesota bishop, tells NPR ‘I won’t apologize’ for sermon addressing Trump
item.description: The Episcopal bishop of Washington spoke with NPR to discuss her sermon addressing President Trump and why she asked him to have mercy.
item.dc:creator: Alana Wise, MPR News
